title: (P34) immunostaining of free floating sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f8aae","text":"This is a protocol for performing routine immunostainings of free floating sections. It includes 2 optional sections for enhancing weak signals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Anesthetize animals 0.1 mL 2.5% Avertin per 10 g bodyweight i.p. using a sterile tuberculin syringe (26g needle).
Perfuse with 10 mL ice cold 1X PBS.
Perfuse with 10 mL ice cold 4% PFA.
Postfix the sample in 4% PFA 4 °C Overnight with gentle agitation.
Microwave 00:01:00 , or until boiling (depends on the power).
Transfer the brain sections back to the multi-well plate if the antigen retrival step is performed.
Dilute Primary antibody in PBST 1:1000.
Incubate the sections with the diluted primary antibodies at room temperature Overnight with gentle agitation. Each well of 24 well plate uses 100uL.
Wash PBST 5min X3.
Dilute Secondary antibody in PBST 1:1000.
Incubate the sections with the diluted secondary antibodies at room temperature Overnight with gentle agitation. Each well of 24 well plate uses 100uL.
Wash PBST 5min X3.
Mount in Ymount and image.
Deactivate endogenous peroxidase activity with 3 % (v/v) H 2 O 2 in PBST 01:00:00 with gentle agitation.
Wash with PBST 00:05:00 .
Dilute reagent A and reagent B 1:50 in PBST.
Mix and RT 00:30:00 .
Add A B mix to sample, 00:30:00 (Optional overnight for complete penetration).
Wash with PBST 00:05:00 . repeat 3 times.
Incubate with 1X fluorescently conjugated TSA reagent with 0.0015% H2O2 in PBST in dark, 00:02:00 .
Wash with HRP stop buffer 00:05:00 X3.
Wash with PBST 00:05:00 .
Mount in Ymount and image.
Section into 50 µm with a vibratome.
Store the samples at 4 °C in 1X PBS supplied with 1 µL of 4% PFA. The samples can be stored for upto 1 week.
Wait until the citrate buffer cools down to Room temperature .
Place the sections into 24 well plate (for serial sectioning), or 6 well plate (selected sections without serial sectioning).
Make a mark on one side of the brain. This can be done by cutting a small piece off on one side, depending on what brain region will be used.
All experiment involving the use of live animals must be reviewed and approved by the IACUC, and comply with NIH and institutional guideline.
Weight the animal.
Melt 4% low melting point agarose with a microwave briefly and keep it at 37 °C afterwards.
Pour a thin layer of melt agarose to the peel-a-way mold.
Keep the mold in 4 °C until it turns solid.

Embed the brain sample in the mold with the melt agarose. The A-P axis should be vertical to one edge of the mold. The brain is placed upside down so that bragma and lambda are in contact of the surface.
This is an optional step to enhance antibody binding. It is recommended for antibodies with weak signals or no signal.
Transfer the brain sections into a beaker with enough citrate buffer to cover all sections. e.g., 20 sections in a 500 mL beaker filled with 100 mL citrate buffer.
This step can further enhance weak signals. The secondary antibody must be conjugated with biotin.
creator: Yunming Wu
